LAW AND MOTION 301, JAN-09-2026 RE: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION OF RECONSIDERATION OF MINUTE ORDER GRANTED DEFEANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS DENIED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST PROVIDE A PAPER COPY TO DEPT. 301 WITH PROOF OF COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Reconsideration Of Minute Order Granted Defeant'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof, Plaintiff'S Declaration
Off Calendar Jan-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ARTSIOM KHATKEVICH's Motion Of Reconsideration Of Minute Order Granted Defeant'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons.

Appearances:
Artsiom Khatkevich, in court, Plaintiff in propria persona.
Olena Kats, via Zoom videoconference, specially appearing Defendant in propria persona.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for reconsideration is denied. A motion for reconsideration must rely on "new or different facts, circumstances, or law." (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 1008, subd. (a).) A motion for reconsideration does not lie where it is based on simple disagreement with a prior order. Plaintiff's motion provides no facts, circumstances, or law that could not have been presented in opposition to the motion.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must provide a paper copy to Dept. 301 with proof of compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Complaint Under Ccp 430.10 To All Parties And Their Attorneys Of Record
Off Calendar Nov-26-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT OLENA KATS' Motion To Dismiss Complaint Under Ccp 430.10 To All Parties And Their Attorneys Of Record.

Appearances:
Artsiom Khatkevich, in person, in propria persona, Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

The matter is briefly argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling, without prejudice, as amended in court. Dropped from calendar. 1. Defendant failed to provide a meet and confer declaration in compliance with CCP 430.41(a). 2. Opposition to motions was also untimely.

the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Barbara Zuniga. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (302/BZ)
